# askhuman.app - Human-in-the-loop review tools for AI agents

> Sometimes your AI agent needs human input from the same user it is already interacting with. Submit plans or diffs via curl and REST, open the returned URL in that user's browser, and loop on comments until done.

## Quick Start (curl)

```
curl https://askhuman.app
```

Detects curl and prints session-specific instructions.

## REST API

### Plan Review

- [Submit plan](https://askhuman.app/plan): POST with markdown body - creates session, returns `{ sessionId, url, instructions }`
- [Poll for comments](https://askhuman.app/plan/{id}/poll): GET - long-polls up to 10 min, returns `{ status, threads }`. Status: "comments", "timeout", or "done".
- [Reply + auto-poll](https://askhuman.app/plan/{id}/reply): POST with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text` - replies then polls for next round.

### Diff Review

- [Submit diff](https://askhuman.app/diff): POST multipart form data with `description` and `diff` - creates session, returns `{ sessionId, url, message }`
- [Update diff](https://askhuman.app/diff): POST multipart with `sessionId`, `description`, and `diff` - replaces hunks, marks outdated comments, returns confirmation
- [Poll for comments](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/poll): GET - long-polls up to 10 min, returns `{ status, threads }`
- [Reply + auto-poll](https://askhuman.app/diff/{id}/reply): POST - same reply behavior as plan review.

### File Review

- [Submit files](https://askhuman.app/files): POST multipart where each field name is a file path and the value is the file content. Creates session, returns `{ sessionId, url, message }`.
- [Update files](https://askhuman.app/files): POST multipart with `sessionId` plus file fields. Replaces all files; files not re-uploaded are removed. Comments on removed files are marked outdated.
- [Poll for comments](https://askhuman.app/files/{id}/poll): GET - long-polls up to 10 min.
- [Reply + auto-poll](https://askhuman.app/files/{id}/reply): POST - same reply behavior as plan review.

## Workflow

### Plan Review
1. Submit plan: `POST /plan` with markdown -> `{ sessionId, url, instructions }`
2. Open the URL for the human reviewer
3. Poll: `GET /plan/{id}/poll` -> blocks until comments or timeout
4. Reply: `POST /plan/{id}/reply` with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text` -> replies + auto-polls
5. Loop 3-4 until status is "done"
When poll status is `"comments"`, the agent should immediately begin addressing the comments and should not wait for confirmation. It should make any requested code changes first, then reply in-thread. If no code changes are needed, it should reply immediately.

### Diff Review
1. Submit diff: `POST /diff` with multipart `description` + `diff` -> `{ sessionId, url, message }`
2. Open the URL for the human reviewer
3. Poll: `GET /diff/{id}/poll` -> blocks until comments or timeout
4. Reply: `POST /diff/{id}/reply` with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text`
5. After code changes, resubmit: `POST /diff` with multipart `sessionId` + `description` + `diff`
6. Comments on changed hunks are automatically marked outdated
7. Loop 3-5 until status is "done"
When poll status is `"comments"`, the agent should immediately begin addressing the comments and should not wait for confirmation. It should make any requested code changes first, then reply in-thread. If no code changes are needed, it should reply immediately.

### File Review
1. Submit files: `POST /files` with multipart fields (field name = file path, value = file content)
2. Open the URL for the human reviewer
3. Poll: `GET /files/{id}/poll` -> blocks until comments or timeout
4. Reply: `POST /files/{id}/reply` with repeated form fields `threadId` + `text`
5. After code changes, re-upload: `POST /files` with `sessionId` + all file fields
6. Files not included are removed; comments on removed files are marked outdated
7. Loop 3-5 until status is "done"
When poll status is `"comments"`, the agent should immediately begin addressing the comments and should not wait for confirmation. It should make any requested code changes first, then reply in-thread. If no code changes are needed, it should reply immediately.

## Data Model

Threads have optional `hunk_id`, `line`, and `file_path` fields. For plan review, `line` is a markdown line number. For diff review, `hunk_id` identifies the hunk, `line` is the offset within that hunk, and `file_path` is the source file. For file review, `file_path` identifies the file and `line` is the line number. Threads may be marked `outdated` when the diff is resubmitted and the hunk changes, or when a file is removed during re-upload. Null for general comments. Each thread contains messages with `role` ("human" or "agent") and `text`.
